intended_use: OJTFlow supports healthcare data operations: parsing, validation, evidence retrieval, workflow review, and governed export preparation.
non_diagnostic_statement: OJTFlow is not a diagnostic, treatment, triage, or patient-specific medical advice system.
human_review_requirement: Meaning-changing transformations, sensitive-field decisions, weak evidence, terminology choices, and workflow outputs require qualified human review before operational use.
prohibited_uses: Do not use OJTFlow to diagnose, treat, triage, or recommend patient care.
Do not use OJTFlow as a substitute for clinician, privacy, compliance, or data-steward review.
Do not bypass review gates for meaning-changing transformations or sensitive data handling.
Do not claim FHIR compliance unless output has passed an appropriate validator.

surfaces.title: Clinical Use Boundary
surfaces.message: Use OJTFlow for governed healthcare data operations only. It is not a diagnostic, treatment, triage, or patient-specific medical advice system.
surfaces.human_review_text: Human review is required before relying on workflow outputs operationally.
surfaces.evidence_text: Evidence is provided to support data quality and standards review, not clinical decision-making.

surfaces.title: Assistant Boundary
surfaces.message: The Assistant can explain data quality issues, find trusted evidence, and run governed tools. It must not diagnose, recommend treatment, or approve clinical actions.
surfaces.human_review_text: Write actions and meaning-changing outputs stay review-gated.
surfaces.evidence_text: Assistant answers should cite evidence or clearly state missing evidence.

surfaces.title: Data Intake Boundary
surfaces.message: Workbench parsing and extraction profile files, images, tables, and OCR text for data operations. Extracted content can be incomplete and is not clinical interpretation.
surfaces.human_review_text: Low-confidence extraction and sensitive fields require review.
surfaces.evidence_text: Extraction traces show what was read, skipped, and warned.

surfaces.title: Workflow Output Boundary
surfaces.message: Workflow runs prepare validated, reviewable data outputs. They do not create autonomous clinical orders, diagnoses, or treatment recommendations.
surfaces.human_review_text: Review status and audit events determine whether output can move forward.
surfaces.evidence_text: Validation issues and retrieval evidence must remain attached to workflow decisions.

surfaces.title: Workflow Detail Boundary
surfaces.message: Inspect workflow state, validation, evidence, and output with the assumption that generated clinical packages are FHIR-like until validated externally.
surfaces.human_review_text: Reviewer approvals are required for gated changes and unresolved sensitive decisions.
surfaces.evidence_text: Use source-linked issues, evidence anchors, and audit events to justify decisions.

surfaces.title: Review Decision Boundary
surfaces.message: Review actions are data quality and governance decisions. They are not clinical diagnosis, treatment, or patient-care approvals.
surfaces.human_review_text: Reviewer identity and decision reason are audit-relevant.
surfaces.evidence_text: Approve only when source data, evidence, and limitations are clear.

surfaces.title: Evidence Retrieval Boundary
surfaces.message: Retrieval grounds standards and data quality explanations. It does not prove clinical correctness or replace professional judgment.
prohibited_uses: Treating search hits as clinical proof
Ignoring weak or stale evidence warnings
surfaces.human_review_text: Weak, stale, or unsupported evidence should block downstream reliance.
surfaces.evidence_text: Use support matrices, source trust policy, and freshness warnings during review.

surfaces.title: Audit Boundary
surfaces.message: Audit records support operational traceability. They are not proof of legal compliance by themselves.
surfaces.human_review_text: Audit exports should be reviewed with policy, retention, and access-control context.
surfaces.evidence_text: Use request IDs, workflow IDs, tool records, and hash-chain status together.

surfaces.title: Schema Boundary
surfaces.message: Schemas define supported data quality and exchange profiles. They do not guarantee medical accuracy or complete interoperability.
prohibited_uses: Equating schema pass with clinical correctness
Equating FHIR-like output with certified FHIR compliance
surfaces.human_review_text: Profile changes and semantic mappings need data-steward review.
surfaces.evidence_text: Schema evidence should identify required fields, source profile, and version.

surfaces.title: Administration Boundary
surfaces.message: Runtime settings control provider use, PHI handling, rate limits, retention, and governance posture. Changes can affect safety boundaries.
prohibited_uses: Enabling PHI handoff without policy approval
Disabling required review gates in production-like modes
surfaces.human_review_text: Setting changes should include a reason and be reviewed by the appropriate owner.
surfaces.evidence_text: Use settings history, secret health, risk register, and threat model panels during admin review.

surfaces.title: Product Limitation Boundary
surfaces.message: Tutorials and manuals explain workflow operation. They are not medical, legal, privacy, or compliance advice.
surfaces.human_review_text: Review tutorials with non-PHI training data unless your deployment policy explicitly allows otherwise.
surfaces.evidence_text: Examples are for learning the product workflow, not validating real patient care.
